warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.sourceTitle: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015
rules.notes: The full standard GB 2760-2015 must be consulted for specific additive types, maximum levels, and application scope.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015

rules.sourceTitle: Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) Online Database
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Specific provisions are available in the searchable online database, and users are encouraged to consult the Preamble for interpretation.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods

rules.notes: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food commodity is the maximum concentration legally permitted. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels, which are detailed in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.notes: A Code of Practice (CXC 78-2017) exists for the prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices. This document should be consulted for specific guidance.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXC 78-2017 Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices

rules.notes: The EU Additives Database provides information on approved food additives and their conditions of use, based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii), their toxins and metabolites (e.g., staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites in specific foods, and also process hygiene criteria.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية - الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال
rules.notes: This regulation outlines the general requirements for food to be considered Halal, applicable to seasoning, snack, and processed foods making Halal claims.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 2055-1:2015 لائحة فنية خليجية --> الأغذية الحلال – الجزء الأول : الاشتراطات العامة للأغذية الحلال

rules.notes: This regulation outlines the general requirements for labelling of prepackaged food products, which includes seasoning and snack products.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.notes: This regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for all food products, including seasoning and snack products.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية - فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية
rules.notes: This standard provides guidelines for optional shelf life periods for food products, including seasoning and snack products.

rules.parameter: Ingredients, production, processing, use, cooking, or preservation methods for food or additives
rules.limit: Verification required against MHLW established criteria/standards
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act and Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc.
rules.notes: Specific criteria and standards are established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. Refer to 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act' for details.
rules.evidenceQuote: the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish the criteria for the methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives... or may establish standards for the ingredients of food or additives... food or additives that do not conform to such standards must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, or sold.

rules.parameter: Unapproved additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed items used as additives)
rules.limit: Prohibited, unless MHLW specifies as unlikely to cause harm
rules.notes: This implies a positive list system for approved additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and things that have generally been served for human consumption, and are used as additives) and preparations and food containing additives must not be sold... except for cases that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.limit: Prohibited if likely to cause harm due to contamination or addition of foreign substances
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds

rules.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, and pharmaceutical products for animals
rules.notes: This refers to the 'Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues in Food'.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food in which substances that are the ingredients of agricultural chemicals... feed... and pharmaceutical products... remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health... must not be produced, imported, processed, used, cooked, preserved, for the purpose of sale, or sold

rules.parameter: Standards for apparatus, containers, packaging, or their raw materials, and production methods
rules.limit: Verification required against MHLW established criteria/standards
rules.sourceTitle: Food Sanitation Act and Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc.
rules.notes: Specific standards for apparatus, containers, packaging, or their raw materials, and production methods are established by the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare. Refer to 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act' for details.
rules.evidenceQuote: the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish standards for apparatus, or containers and packaging, or the raw materials thereof... or may establish the criteria for the production methods thereof... the apparatus, or containers and packaging that does not conform to the standards must not be sold, produced or imported for the purpose of sale, or used in business

rules.limit: Prohibited, unless specified by MHLW as unlikely to cause harm
rules.evidenceQuote: those which contain or bear toxic or harmful substances or are suspected to contain or bear such substances; provided, however, that this does not apply when the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies that the substances are unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Toxic or harmful substances in apparatus, containers, and packaging
rules.limit: Prohibited if likely to cause harm to human health or have a harmful effect on food/additives
rules.evidenceQuote: Apparatus, or containers and packaging which contains or bears toxic or harmful substances and is likely to cause harm to human health, or apparatus, or containers and packaging which comes into contact with food or additives and has a harmful effect on them and which is likely to cause harm to human health must not be sold, produced or imported for the purpose of sale, or used in business.

rules.limit: Prohibited if contaminated or suspected to be contaminated and likely to cause harm
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are contaminated with pathogens or are suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
rules.limit: Not to be directly added during manufacturing of kueh; permitted in fillings and toppings within prescribed limits
rules.notes: This rule is specific to the manufacturing and use of preservatives in kuehs.
rules.evidenceQuote: Class II chemical preservatives, such as sorbic acid and benzoic acid, should not be directly added when manufacturing the kueh. Class II chemical preservatives can only be directly added, within the prescribed regulatory limits, to fillings and toppings in kuehs.

rules.notes: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with regulatory limits on food additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.limit: Must be assessed and permitted by SFA
rules.notes: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.limit: Calculation tool must be used to determine permitted levels
rules.notes: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are used, the SFA Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives must be used.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.notes: Food products containing an additive that is not permitted must not be imported or sold.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.notes: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.notes: Food businesses must check the List of Permitted Food Additives or use the Food Additives Search tool.
rules.evidenceQuote: Download the List of Permitted Food Additives or use the Food Additives Search tool to check if your additives are allowed.

rules.notes: Different limits apply depending on the additive and food product type; refer to Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Food businesses must be aware of commonly found contaminants in their products and conduct laboratory tests as needed.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food businesses must be aware of the contaminants that are commonly found in their food products and conduct laboratory tests as needed.

rules.limit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Contaminants in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Food or food products containing contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed regulatory limits must not be imported or sold.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.parameter: Various contaminants (e.g., heavy metals, mycotoxins, pesticide residues)
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Specific limits for different types of contaminants require reference to SFA's detailed documents (Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents).
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below: Microbiological standards, Heavy metals, Mycotoxins and Marine biotoxins, Veterinary drug residues, Pesticide residues, Other incidental constituents.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Maximum Residue Limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues in various food commodities must be complied with; refer to the SFA website for the list.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.limit: Must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels
rules.notes: Applies to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.notes: Prepacked food products must comply with general labelling requirements.
rules.evidenceQuote: Your food or food product must also comply with the labelling requirements.

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Specific microbiological standards for ready-to-eat food require reference to SFA's detailed documents.
rules.evidenceQuote: Microbiological standards: Ready-to-eat food

rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food is not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).
rules.evidenceQuote: Ready-to-eat (RTE) food are not required to be tested for total plate count (TPC).

rules.parameter: γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) produced by microbial fermentation
rules.sourceTitle: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient
rules.notes: This regulation specifies use restrictions and labeling requirements for γ-Aminobutyric Acid (GABA) as a food ingredient.
rules.evidenceQuote: Use Restrictions and Labeling Requirements of γ-Aminobutyric Acid Produced by Microbial Fermentation as a Food Ingredient

rules.parameter: Cephalopods and cephalopod products; Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof; Seeds and seed products (excluding highly refined sunflower seed oil); Kiwis and kiwi products thereof
rules.limit: Recommended declaration if present as ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: These allergens are recommended for labeling if present as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive: (1) Cephalopods and cephalopod products... (2) Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof... (3) Seeds and seed products... (4) Kiwis and kiwi products thereof

rules.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
rules.limit: Mandatory declaration if present as ingredient or food additive
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: These 10 allergens require mandatory labeling on prepackaged foods if present as an ingredient or food additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive... Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish

rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: Mandatory declaration if the residue sulphur dioxide concentration is 10 mg/kg or more.
rules.evidenceQuote: sulphite as the residue sulphur dioxide for 10 or more milligrams per kilogram of food.

rules.sourceTitle: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods
rules.notes: Specific limits for pesticide residues in foods are detailed in the referenced standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standards for Pesticide Residue Limits in Foods

rules.sourceTitle: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products
rules.notes: Specific requirements for nutrition claims on prepackaged food products are detailed in the referenced regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Revised Regulations on Nutrition Claim for Prepackaged Food Products

rules.limit: Suggested statement if risk of cross-contamination exists
rules.sourceTitle: Regulations Governing Food Allergen Labeling on the Recommended Labeling Allergens
rules.notes: A statement such as 'The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○' is suggested.
rules.evidenceQuote: If there is a risk of a food product being affected by allergen cross-contamination... the following statement of precautionary allergen labeling is suggested: “ The plant, equipment or production line used to produce the product is also used to process○○” or the synonymous terms.

rules.sourceTitle: กองอาหาร (Food Division)
rules.notes: Specific regulations for food additives must be consulted under 'วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร'.
rules.evidenceQuote: วัตถุเจือปนอาหาร

rules.evidenceQuote: การแสดงฉลากอาหารและฉลากโภชนาการ

rules.evidenceQuote: อาหารที่ห้ามผลิต นำเข้า หรือจำหน่าย

rules.evidenceQuote: มาตรฐานอาหารด้านจุลินทรีย์ที่ทำให้เกิดโรค

rules.limit: Verification required against FDA regulations and inventories
rules.notes: Food and color additives must be approved and listed in FDA regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Approved Food and Color Additives and Petitions Under Review

rules.limit: Must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173, 73, 74). Verification required.
rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: The Substances Added to Food inventory includes these, but it is a partial list.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively)

rules.limit: Must be listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184). Verification required.
rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: The Substances Added to Food inventory includes these, but it is a partial list.
rules.evidenceQuote: Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184).

rules.limit: Prohibited from use in food, as listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189). Verification required.
rules.sourceTitle: Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS) | FDA
rules.notes: Substances listed as "PROHIBITED" or "PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS".
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food (labeled as "PROHIBITED" or "PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS").

rules.notes: The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.notes: This is a Guidance Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm CPG 500.500

rules.notes: This is an Action Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb CPG 555.400

rules.notes: This is an Action Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Peanuts and peanut products Action Level 20 ppb CPG 570.375

rules.notes: This is an Action Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Pistachio nuts Action Level 20 ppb CPG 570.500

rules.limit: Verification required against the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool and relevant guidance documents/regulations.
rules.notes: Includes tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA has published a variety of contaminant levels for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food in various guidance documents and regulations for industry. For ease of reference, the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels.

rules.limit: Verification required against FDA final rules for "gluten-free" definition and compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods.
rules.notes: FDA issued a final rule defining "gluten-free" for food labeling and compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling... On August 12, 2020, the FDA issued a final rule to establish compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods, or foods that contain fermented or hydrolyzed ingredients, bearing the “gluten-free” claim.

rules.notes: Such statements are not required by law and should only be used to address unavoidable "cross-contact" after good manufacturing practices have been incorporated. They must be truthful and not misleading.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact...

rules.notes: The CGMP & PC rule (21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens. Facilities must have written procedures.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA’s “Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food” rule (CGMP & PC rule, 21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements applicable to establishments that manufacture, process, pack, or hold human food. The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens.

rules.notes: Must be declared on the label as they are identified as allergenic substances.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine, which are derived from insects, have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.notes: The food source of a major food allergen must appear in parentheses following the ingredient name OR immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a "contains" statement. Specific types of tree nuts, fish species, and Crustacean shellfish species must be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: FALCPA requires that foods or ingredients that contain a "major food allergen" be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.

rules.notes: Required to be identified on labels due to potential hypersensitivity reactions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels so consumers who are sensitive to the dye can avoid it.

rules.notes: Must be declared on food labels when present at or above the specified concentration.
rules.evidenceQuote: they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.

rules.notes: Specific additive limits for seasoning and snack products must be verified through official documents issued by the Ministry of Health, accessible via the government's legal document portal.
